comment.body:
yes...
and this is why i keep telling:
market cannot be ALWAYS predicted. sometimes it can be with a certain conviction level, then the prediction is either proven wrong or right.
but most of the times it is only levels, targets and structure, that one can use to resize/rebalance his positions, and determine the risk and its conditions.

trading is a dynamic process

comment.body:
market is changing fast, and this change accelerates exponentially.
what i mean by this is that there will be times in the very near futures (when crypto market will be normal, in the next 4-5 years), when only the numbers will matter.
not the actual performance. that will be a prolonged bubble. 
by the AI managed systems and future-tokenized new kind of derivatives, the real performance will be masked as much as it can be...
similarly as it was explained in this famous movie scene, but with even more tranches: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pxr_FzpPM2Q

comment.body:
in 2008, many economist thought that this crisis is the end of the capitalism...
they thought it bcs the derivative market created a big chaos, and nothing makes it impossible to do it again.
the main thing is that there is always more credit out-there than collateral, and it can be artificially masked.
it is the same since Tiberius kaiser.

comment.body:
they weren't wrong in principle terms, but they were wrong in timing.
bcs back then the valuation couldn't be faked this much as it is possible now.
Now you can fake valuation, and bcs the tail and the dog became one, and the circle became full, nothing will reveal it
